title: Replicating Entities
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Replicating Entities To improve performance if there is no single location that can be easily accessed by all relevant processes, it might be necessary to define the same entity more than once. For example, an entity might be accessed from several remote locations via a network with insufficient capacity. In this case, placing the entity at each location that needs to access it improves performance. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
